ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION 503, DEFENDANT METALCLAD INSULATION CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF ISSUES. HEARING HELD AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: DEFENDANT METALCLAD INSULATION CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS OFF CALENDAR AT ITS REQUEST. DEFENDANT'S ALTERNATE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED AS TO ISSUES 1 AND 2 (GENERAL NEGLIGENCE EXCLUDING NEGLIGENT FAILURE TO WARN, STRICT LIABILITY DESIGN AND MANUFACTURING DEFECT), DENIED AS TO ISSUE 4 (PUNITIVE DAMAGES) AND TAKEN OFF CALENDAR AS TO ISSUE 3 (PREMISES OWNER/CONTRACTOR LIABILITY). (SEE CLERK'S MINI MINUTES FOR COMPLETE DETAILS OF RULING.) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: TERI L. JACKSON, REPORTER: CARI L. WATERS-DREWRY OF AIKEN WELCH, CSR #12401

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Oct-02-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Lisa L. Oberg of McKENNA LONG & ALDRIDGE (415-267-4175), appearing for defendant / moving party Metalclad Insulation Corporation
Jennifer L. Alesio of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Benjamin Carter

RULING:
DEFENDANT METALCLAD INSULATION CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF ISSUES

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Metalclad Insulation Corporation's motion for summary judgment is off calendar at its request. Defendant's alternate motion for summary adjudication is granted as to issues 1 and 2 (general negligence excluding negligent failure to warn, strict liability design and manufacturing defect), denied as to issue 4 (punitive damages) and taken off calendar as to issue 3 (premises owner/contractor liability). As to issues 1 and 2, defendant satisfied its burden of production of making a prima facie case that it is entitled to prevail on its government contractor defense as to non-duty to warn claims and plaintiff failed to present evidence creating a triable issue on this defense. As to punitive damages, defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiff does not now possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant punitive damages. Plaintiff's responses to defendant's special interrogatories preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Plaintiff has dismissed his premises owner/contractor liability cause of action. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Cari L. Waters-Drewry of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #12401 (503/TLJ)
